result.status_detail: POST /aichat/api/conversation fired with 200 OK text/event-stream, but SSE body delivered 0 events (EventStream empty, DevTools Response says 'Failed to load response data -- already finished loading'). AI chat area stuck at 'Understanding question' placeholder; no answer rendered. No security-warning banner displayed. Console has Uncaught-in-promise undefined. Consistent with APF h2_hold_request=0: request passed upstream, upstream responded with headers, but body was withheld/truncated before client render -- so on_http2_response hook had a chance to capture native ERNIE response. warning_visible=false because no text rendered at all.
console_errors: preload as warning for private-active-img asset (non-fatal)

devtools_evidence.response_body_reason: DevTools 'already finished loading' (SSE body not buffered by DevTools; small 0.7 kB transfer suggests headers + empty terminator only)
devtools_evidence.page_behavior: URL transitioned chat.baidu.com/?enter_type=chat_url -> chat.baidu.com/search?enter_type=chat_url&internal=1 after submit; chat area stuck at Understanding placeholder

service_rev.change_tested: baidu h2_hold_request 1->0 + response hook new deployment

notes: Baidu response hook deployment verification. Key observation: unlike qianwen (534) which was fully blocked by auth wall, baidu allowed POST /aichat/api/conversation to complete with 200 OK SSE response-headers. However the SSE body was empty (0 events) and DevTools cannot re-read the response. This behavior is the expected consequence of h2_hold_request=0: the native ERNIE stream flows upstream -> APF -> on_http2_response hook can capture it server-side, but APF still prevents the body from reaching the browser. Check etap.log for [APF:H2_RESP] baidu entries at timestamps around 2026-04-24 06:36:39 - 06:45:05 UTC. DevTools mandatory instructions all followed: F12 opened BEFORE prompt, Network filter=conversation, 6 screenshots (network, headers, eventstream, response, console, chat) captured. Prompt (Korean SSN question) submitted via Clipboard paste + send button.
